TextENGNew: The same day, somewhere in Kakiuchi City.
...The dusk coated the park in a blood red hue, casting an eerie shadow on the premises.
(Well... what should I do?)
Ooishi stood up from the park bench and crushed his cigarette under his foot.

Even though he'd learned his suspicions were right on the mark, there was no satisfaction in it. ...Instead, a labored feeling weighed down on his shoulders and pushed on his entire body, making it hard to move.
...It happened completely by coincidence.
He stopped by a restaurant to grab a late lunch, and by chance he noticed the TV broadcasting a daytime drama... with a bulletin message on the bottom of the screen.
"Woman's body found on mountain road in Gifu prefecture, currently identifying."
When he saw that message, Ooishi immediately thought back to the investigation meeting from the other day.
"...It's about the grandmother of the Kimiyoshi family, Aki.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1049. Apparently, nobody has seen her for the past few days."

"So, I asked Touji Kimiyoshi about it the other day.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1051. He confirmed that he traveled to the mountains in Gifu prefecture on Sunday.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1052. He claimed he was out fishing."
"...So you're saying there's a high probability that the suspect was being duplicitous. Right, Hanada-kun?"
Ooishi rushed to contact Kakiuchi Station. But unfortunately all the investigators, including Akasaka, were out heading to the Kimiyoshi household. Madoka told him she was about to leave, too.

Thus, he had no choice but to directly contact the Gifu prefectural police about the woman's head discovered in the mountains... However, they refused to confirm any details on the grounds of confidentiality.
(...It really hurts not having Tomoe-chan around at times like these.)
He knew it was too late now, but he was keenly aware of what a blessing her existence had been, and was frustrated by how vertically split the police departments had become.
Each station had a limited scope of responsibility, called a "jurisdiction" boundary.
Simply put, it was their territory, and the vast majority of the people in those organizations had little respect for outsiders.
So, even when speaking with a fellow policeman, they'd be tight-lipped with details when dealing with anyone from outside their territory.

Plus, it would still be fine if he could blame himself. ...But as long as he was doing this investigation work through the good will of Kakiuchi Station's Chief Yamaoki, any wrong move Ooishi made would likely cause problems for the station.
He grew weary of this closed, troublesome system many years ago, but as a voluntary member of that society, he had no choice but to endure it.
(...Well, it would be problematic if they wanted all the details, too.)
If he said, "I'd like to cooperate because I believe that's Aki Kimiyoshi's corpse," and they asked for his reasons, how would he respond?
Then if he responded by telling them that she was a former Hinamizawa resident, and may have been caught up in this incident as a victim of the "curse"... what would happen?
It was easy to predict. Naturally, they'd be completely surprised and want to ask him for details about the situation.
Then, the Gifu prefectural police would find out about the investigation that Ooishi and the others were conducting, then convey that information to the higher-ups in command...
"...That wouldn't even be funny."

Even within Chief Yamaoki's Kakiuchi Station, the number of people he was willing to collaborate with was dwindling because they couldn't distinguish ally from enemy. Another station couldn't be trusted.
Indeed, Ooishi had grown suspicious of the Gifu prefectural police since the nurse burning incident a while back.
It was incredibly frustrating that the information he'd spent blood and tears to obtain had amounted to nothing in the face of people like that.
...As a result, Ooishi couldn't reveal his true intentions, and had to rely on vague excuses like, "...It resembles a person from a search case."
But naturally, he felt like he'd been snubbed, so much that it made him angry enough to wish he could jump to the other end of the line and give those people a piece of his mind...
"Well... it might be faster to ask them directly.@k@vS08/11/321100730. Nfu fu fu..."
After all, indirect attacks weren't Ooishi's field of expertise.
He had his fair share of confidence when it came to gradually cornering suspects while cutting off their path of retreat in one fell swoop.
Either way... it was no longer the time to kick back and watch as things developed.
When Ooishi first contacted Natsumi Kimiyoshi, he saw her as an unfortunate victim who'd gotten mixed up in these incidents.
However, after learning her past, and discovering that her grandmother Aki Kimiyoshi may have fallen into the worst possible situation, he began to think it was the opposite.

Natsumi Kimiyoshi almost certainly had some involvement in the incident, or at least knowledge of the circumstances. ...And, if Tomoe's words were right, she looked completely different beneath the surface.
So, to prevent any further tragedies, securing her as soon as possible was the top priority.

It was probably going to be some time before the Gifu prefectural police determined the identity of "Aki Kimiyoshi." So they wouldn't have a warrant to search the Kimiyoshi house until then, giving them all the more reason.
That time lag was truly their last opportunity. If he squandered his time, it would be too late...
"...Guess I should get going."
He began walking down this path for the second time.
Last time he came, he was struck by a sudden downpour and had to hurry out of there.
That was when he met Natsumi for the first time, at the steps in front of the Kimiyoshi house...
(...At the time, I thought she was just an ordinary girl.)
He had no way of knowing she was such a problematic child back in Okinomiya... Ooishi simply didn't have that information available to him.
Even if she was a problem child, those were nothing unusual. Unless she'd gone on a rampage or formed a clique, the police would have had no reason to get involved, and thus wouldn't be remembered.
However, when it came to skipping school... in other words, lurking in the shadows, those kinds of kids were elusive.
It would be exhausting to keep a constant eye on how kids acted in school in the first place.

If anything... it was no concern of theirs what sickness she was being hospitalized for, or what treatment was being done. That sort of strict observation would make them an information-controlled totalitarian state.
"But, well... after a problem like this occurs, you start jumping at shadows."
...He suddenly muttered to himself, trying to think.
The people behind the "curse" probably wanted to disseminate fear to the general public, so maybe they saw Natsumi Kimiyoshi as an ideal target?
At least on the surface, Natsumi looked like an ordinary girl without any problems.

If even a child like that got caught up in these tragedies caused by former Hinamizawa residents... or if she herself caused one of said tragedies, how would it change people's perception of Hinamizawa...?
Maybe people would see them like a bomb about to go off, and avoid them like the plague. It could lead to discrimination and persecution as well.
And then, the people from Hinamizawa would feel even more isolated from the world, which would drive them into hiding... burying the truth behind this "curse" and the disaster forever.
(Well played... shrewd bastards.)
As soon as the thought came to him... he let out a sigh.
But what was the reason to make such a flashy display of the "curse" and then silence the voices of the nonbelievers?
A total of 2,000 casualties seemed just too much... but Ooishi-san couldn't comprehend why these people were trying to treat incidents in a small rural town like a major social phenomenon.
He suddenly turned his gaze to the fountain in the park and noticed a girl in a familiar uniform talking to a male student.
Even from this distance, that hairstyle was unmistakable. ...Natsumi Kimiyoshi.
And the boy beside her...
(Right... from back then.)
It was the boy who rescued her at the coffee shop on that rainy day a week ago.
A boy with a strong glow in his eyes. ...Ooishi was sure that Natsumi Kimiyoshi referred to him as "Toudou-kun."
The conversation between the two of them echoed through the park... and with nobody else around, Ooishi managed to hear some of it from a decent distance away.
"...It's fine, Toudou-kun.@k@vS08/36/320100361. Really... it's nothing."
"Kimiyoshi... please talk to me.@k@vS08/39/320200066. I... don't want to hear any more lies."
"...Ah, ahahaha.@k@vS08/36/320100363. In that case, there's nothing left for me to say..."
Natsumi evasively laughed at the boy with a serious look on his face. ...Even without noticing Ooishi's approach, the two of them stood there in the tense atmosphere.
...Based on the bags sitting by their feet, the two of them were probably on the way home from school.
And it was clear from the atmosphere that these two weren't just classmates, they were close.
And... Ooishi realized that their closeness was contributing to the tense air that was forming between the two of them.
Unable to find the words to speak to each other... the silence continued.
Behind the silent air hid a truth that each of them wanted to hear.
...But they couldn't bring themselves to ask. And they didn't want to say anything more.
Sensing that hesitation and rejection fluttering about, Ooishi made up his mind and approached the two of them.
"...Oh, pardon me.@k@vS08/11/321100738. Good evening, Kimiyoshi-san."
When she finally sensed another person and looked back toward Ooishi... Natsumi's complexion completely changed.
And as he approached, she started looking more and more upset.
"Sorry for interrupting.@k@vS08/11/321100740. I'm sorry about before. I'm Ooishi.@k@vS08/11/321100741. Ooishi from the Okinomiya bookstore."
Natsumi's shoulders were trembling. ...So the boy, Akira Toudou, stood in front of Ooishi to protect her.
"Oh... you're the student from the other day.@k@vS08/11/321100743. Am I disturbing you, by any chance?"
"Do you need something?"
"Hmm~, nothing in particular...@k@vS08/11/321100745. from you."
That obvious provocation brought a look of fury to Akira's face.
(He's young. But even so...)
Standing behind Akira, Natsumi looked unusually frightened.
She had originally given off a meek impression... but it was far worse than when he met her before.
Something that happened in the past week might have had a major influence on her... She was starting to look completely unstable... unable to handle the situation.
No doubt. This girl was hiding something about the incident, and about herself...
"Has anything changed since back then?"
"...Oh, that's right.@k@vS08/11/321100749. Actually... I picked up something in this park earlier.@k@vS08/11/321100750. ...Here."
Ooishi held out the small pill case... and when Natsumi saw it, she lunged forward and snatched it away with a surprised expression on her face.
...Faced with that reaction, Ooishi and Akira both looked back at her in shock.
"...Kimiyoshi?"
"Oh my... it was yours, after all.@k@vS08/11/321100752. I was thinking about delivering it to the police since I noticed the familiar name... So, I'm glad.@k@vS08/11/321100753. Nfu fu fu..."
"Is that all you wanted?@k@vS08/39/320200074. In that case, can we leave now?@k@vS08/39/320200075. I have plans to go out with Kimiyoshi after this."
Despite a little bewilderment on his face, Akira confronted Ooishi again out of concern for Natsumi.
Standing behind him, she look significantly more pale than earlier, and she was shivering.

"When I asked around at her school and her neighborhood, people described her as a shy and docile girl... so I don't understand.@k@vS08/28/320500986. It's really surprising that she raised her voice and became aggressive like that."

Ooishi thought back to what Tomoe said and took another look at Natsumi... but there was no trace of that to be found in her now. Ooishi didn't think Tomoe had been lying... but he still found it hard to believe.
Even though Ooishi had learned that she was originally a problem child, no one had ever testified that she was the kind of child capable of snapping.
She was more the timid type, who would shut herself in and say nothing.
In that case, what on earth was the "Natsumi" that Tomoe saw...?
"Well, if I'm in your way I'll step aside.@k@vS08/11/321100756. ...By the way, I passed in front of your house earlier...@k@vS08/11/321100757. and the wall looked like it had been painted over."

"Did there use to be some kind of graffiti there?@k@vS08/11/321100759. There have been a lot of those around here lately... houses with writing on the walls.@k@vS08/11/321100760. Have you seen them on TV or in a magazine?"
Ooishi offered a smile while carefully studying Natsumi's reaction.
For a person capable of snapping, that obstinate way of talking would be infuriating.
It wouldn't be too out of place here... to catch a glimpse of her "alternate" face, like Akasaka and Tomoe were talking about.
However...
"Those ritualistic charms... I wonder who put them there?@k@vS08/11/321100762. Maybe it was your family..."
Natsumi's trembling was growing more pronounced.
...Looking at her reaction, it was clear that she wanted to deny it, but she withdrew in on herself more and more.
"Oh, that's right.@k@vS08/11/321100764. How is your Grandma doing?@k@vS08/11/321100765. The neighbors have been worried."
"T-that's...!"
"...They haven't seen her around... for the past few days.@k@vS08/11/321100767. Did she go somewhere?"
"...Kimiyoshi?"
Akira looked concerned as he turned towards Natsumi. But she stepped back as if in denial, shaking her head several times as her lips quivered.
"T... Toudou-kun... I... I..."
"...?! Kimiyoshi!"
But then, Natsumi rushed away and escaped in no time.
...Even Ooishi couldn't help but feel a little bewildered by that reaction.
(...No different from last time, after all.)

Even though she was in the presence of her classmate... or perhaps even her boyfriend, if she truly had a violent side lurking behind all this, it was hard to believe she could keep it hidden all this time.
And if Natsumi was patient enough to do that, she shouldn't have been capable of snapping in the first place.
Plus, Ooishi didn't get the impression she had a cunning enough personality for that...
(In that case... maybe I overdid it again.)
He scratched his head, struggling to reconcile Tomoe's story and Akasaka's reasoning with what he saw.
For now... Ooishi thought about following her home to hear a more detailed story, but Akira stretched out his hands to stop him.
"Hmm~?@k@vS08/11/321100771. What are you trying to do?"
"Na ha ha ha!@k@vS08/11/321100773. Wow, I can't tell if you're brave or just don't know your place.@k@vS08/11/321100774. ...Can't you understand when someone's telling you to beat it, punk?"
"Y-you're scaring her!@k@vS08/39/320200080. Can you please leave her alone?!"
Finally starting to get annoyed by Akira's presence, Ooishi tried to intimidate him... but even though Akira was truly frightened, he mustered his courage and stood up to Ooishi.
Maybe he was about to receive a beating... but he stretched out his entire body to try and stop him.
When Ooishi recognized the strength of his spirit, he realized that Akira was more than just a classmate.
"...Then, I'll ask you.@k@vS08/11/321100777. Why is Kimiyoshi-san so afraid of me?"
"I have no idea.@k@vS08/39/320200082. ...Anyway, I'm the one who wants to ask the questions here.@k@vS08/39/320200083. Just... who the hell are you?"
"Nfu fu fu.@k@vS08/11/321100779. I'm a publisher..."
"Liar!"
Akira raised his voice as he glared at Ooishi.
As if... he held a child in his hands and was crying out to protect it from death. ...Ooishi could never hate such a passionate young man.
"In that case,@k@vS08/11/321100781. who the hell do you think I am?"
"...A gangster... or something..."
"A... gangster...?@k@vS08/11/321100783. Na ha ha ha!@k@vS08/11/321100784. That's what I look like?"
Akira continued staring at Ooishi and refused to move.
Apparently, that's how things looked.
(...Sheesh, to think I'd be seen in the same light as my mortal enemies, of all things.)
Deep down, he felt a little disappointed.
"Unfortunately, that's not the case.@k@vS08/11/321100787. ...This is how it is."
When Akira looked at the police badge Ooishi held up... tension ran across his face.
His eyes flashed with despair, as if to say he'd expected this... or rather, that his dreaded worst-case scenario had arrived.
"You're a student at Kimiyoshi-san's school, right?@k@vS08/11/321100789. May I ask your name?"
"...Toudou.@k@vS08/39/320200089. Akira Toudou."
"Akira Toudou-san... eh?"
After taking a deep breath and scribbling in his notebook, Ooishi took a seat at the nearest park bench. Akira remained standing and looked down at Ooishi as he lit a cigarette.
"Why... Why is a detective chasing Kimiyoshi-san?"
"Well... why, indeed?"
Ooishi-san deliberately offered an ambiguous response and had a smile on his face as he studied Akira's reaction. ...Ooishi wasn't sure how much the boy knew about Natsumi, so he was gauging him.
Then,
"Is it because... Kimiyoshi is from Hinamizawa?"
Akira didn't sound too angry when the word "Hinamizawa" slipped out. Once Ooishi heard that, he sat up straight and looked up at him with a content expression.
"...I see.@k@vS08/11/321100793. So you already know about that."
".......@k@vS08/39/320200093. I don't know the details.@k@vS08/39/320200094. I only heard that she was from the town next door, Okinomiya..."
"Did you know that detail... about the Kimiyoshi family house's fence?"
"No...@k@vS08/39/320200096. When I went there before, umm...@k@vS08/39/320200097. the Kimiyoshi house and the yard around it... had these strange decorations..."
"So you saw it?@k@vS08/11/321100796. ...Back when it was like that."
Akira had a bitter expression on his face as he lightly nodded. After he saw the house, he thought it might have been better if he hadn't.
"In that case... Toudou-san.@k@vS08/11/321100798. Out of respect for your courage, I'll tell you everything I can.@k@vS08/11/321100799. ...What would you like to know about her?"
".......@k@vS08/39/320200100. What is the relationship between Oyashiro-sama of Hinamizawa... and the Kimiyoshi family?"
Ooishi remained silent as he crushed his cigarette under his foot... then he slowly turned to face Akira.
Originally, he came here intending to provoke Natsumi Kimiyoshi and press her for answers... but she just hid inside her shell, so he didn't get anywhere.
In that case, his only choice was to either persuade her for information or draw a confession out of her, but he couldn't imagine she'd open her heart to a man she feared or even hated.
In that case... what if he tried urging her to change her heart through one of her friends...?
(That's right. This kid... might be able to get through to her.)
With those expectations buried in the bottom of his heart, Ooishi decided to tell him the truth.
"Then, let's talk.@k@vS08/11/321100803. This is only because you're a special friend of Natsumi Kimiyoshi-san.@k@vS08/11/321100804. ...Okay?"
Ooishi lit a second cigarette... After a full inhale and exhale, he looked up at Akira and started speaking.
"...Long ago, Hinamizawa was known by the name "Onigafuchi.""
"Even now, a swamp behind the village bears that name...@k@vS08/11/321100807. and the origin of that name is from a legend purporting the bottom of the swamp is connected to the realm of demons."
"...Realm of demons?"
"And... the surrounding villagers near Onigafuchi feared and respected the demons."
Fear. Respect. ...It was probably closer to worship.
A village where non-humans lived. ...Whether those people saw it as a different world, or as an earthly paradise... was unknown.
"The demons were supposedly capable of performing miracles.@k@vS08/11/321100810. For example, a villager with an untreatable illness was carried to Onigafuchi, and was allegedly cured there."
It matched the image of a tengu, or an immortal mountain wizard.
However...
"But of course, these were man-eating demons.@k@vS08/11/321100812. ...Or so the legends say."
"When a mother brought her son there to receive treatment, they demanded to devour the mother as payment for the service!@k@vS08/11/321100814. Naturally, the mother took her son and ran away."
"As a result... the inhabitants of Onigafuchi all worked together to chase them down."
"The... entire village?"
"That's right.@k@vS08/11/321100817. ...In the end, they caught both the mother and her son... and the demons devoured them both."
Hundreds of them, carrying torches and chasing after them.
As they ran through those dark mountains, scared and alone.
...Just imagining it made his hair stand on end.
As he listened to the story, Akira imagined such a scene. ...He took a deep breath with a mortified look on his face.
"And in the old tales, the villagers were told to never stand in the way when the demons were hunting.@k@vS08/11/321100819. They were told to stay in their homes and hide in their futons."
"What... does that mean?"

"That you should never help or try to hide a person targeted as the demons' prey.@k@vS08/11/321100821. No harm would come to you as long as you didn't get in the way of the demons' hunt.@k@vS08/11/321100822. That was the rule."
"So... what happened to people who broke the rules?"
"Well...@k@vS08/11/321100824. these were man-eating demons, after all.@k@vS08/11/321100825. Nfu fu fu."

As Akira tried to reconcile this new information... he began to tremble. But, he quickly regained his composure after realizing this story had taken their conversation off course. So he reiterated his original question.
"Please, tell me about Kimiyoshi instead of these old stories.@k@vS08/39/320200108. What on earth is all this about Hinamizawa?@k@vS08/39/320200109. And what about Oyashiro-sama?"

"My my, don't be impatient.@k@vS08/11/321100827. These are important details.@k@vS08/11/321100828. In other words... the people from Onigafuchi, now known as Hinamizawa, have a certain special characteristic."
"Especially for the members of the Three Families, who are said to carry it the strongest..."
"Three Families...?"
"...In Hinamizawa, there are three major family lines that carry forward the ancient bloodlines.@k@vS08/11/321100831. Everyone in the village honors and respects them."
"Specifically, the Three Families include the Sonozaki family, the Furude family... and Natsumi-san's own Kimiyoshi family."
"Of course... isn't the mayor of Hinamizawa a member of the Kimiyoshi family?"

"Oh, you knew that?@k@vS08/11/321100834. Furthermore, the Kimiyoshi family wasn't elected to that position. It was a hereditary title.@k@vS08/11/321100835. Their family line has been raising skilled village mayors for generations."
"And for hundreds of years, what seems like forever, they've been the leaders of the village..."
"Hundreds of years...?"
At last.
Akira finally began to understand the significance of the Kimiyoshi family name, and called out in a hoarse voice.

"The villagers have a tremendous amount of trust in them.@k@vS08/11/321100838. Because their family line has been representing the village for so long.@k@vS08/11/321100839. Well... do you know where that trust originated from?"
"Maybe they did a great thing in the past?@k@vS08/39/320200114. ...Or maybe they're rich?"
It was natural to think so.
"Reputation," "wealth," and "personal achievement" were key factors in politics.
However, Ooishi responded "No, no" and took a step toward Akira, then continued.
"Achievements are necessary too, of course...@k@vS08/11/321100841. but in Hinamizawa, what matters most is none other than blood. Their blood."
"...Blood?"
"Given what I was talking about earlier... you should understand what I'm implying, right?"
Akira thought about the story he had just heard and considered what it might indicate.
Then Ooishi pushed forward with an answer of his own.
"You don't get it?@k@vS08/11/321100844. ...In brief, they possess the blood of demons."
Akira raised his head the moment he heard those words.
In other words... Natsumi's body was filled with the blood of those "man-eating demons" he mentioned earlier...?!
"Yes, precisely.@k@vS08/11/321100846. The Kimiyoshi family is one of the families that inherited that blood the strongest.@k@vS08/11/321100847. ...Of course, that includes Natsumi-san."
"So, Kimiyoshi inherited the demon's blood...@k@vS08/39/320200119. Do you truly believe that?"
"Yes, I do.@k@vS08/11/321100849. And that conviction is the reason we're here."
"R-ridiculous..."
"No, it's not ridiculous at all.@k@vS08/11/321100851. In fact, people from Hinamizawa have been causing terrible incidents, one after another."
"The incidents have been getting coverage on TV and in magazines, every day...@k@vS08/11/321100853. You've seen it yourself, haven't you?"
Akira slowly nodded. ...Without those rumors and reports, he never would've been this worried about Natsumi.
"Nfu fu fu.@k@vS08/11/321100855. So, you understand now?@k@vS08/11/321100856. ...Even the people unrelated to these ancient families are causing or becoming involved with these incidents."
"...And if that's the case, then the Kimiyoshi family, which is more closely related, will have it much worse.@k@vS08/11/321100858. ...Wouldn't you agree?"
"Th-then... are you saying that Kimiyoshi might cause a terrible incident?!"
"No, I don't know that.@k@vS08/11/321100860. ...But while I have my suspicions, I need to investigate to confirm.@k@vS08/11/321100861. Do you understand?"
"No way..."
Akira couldn't hide his agitation upon hearing Ooishi's words. He was paralyzed with his eyes open wide.
...But then, he violently shook his head, as if trying to deny that reality.
"...Liar!@k@vS08/39/320200125. Kimiyoshi doing something like that...?!@k@vS08/39/320200126. ...Ridiculous.@k@vS08/39/320200127. That's impossible...!"
Despite saying that, his voice trembled, and there was no strength behind his words. ...Though he thought going further might be cruel, Ooishi asked another question.
"But, at the beginning of all this... you asked if Natsumi-san was from Hinamizawa, right?"

"You wanted to confirm if Natsumi Kimiyoshi was from Hinamizawa, and the implications of that...@k@vS08/11/321100864. because you had your own suspicions about her.@k@vS08/11/321100865. ...Isn't that so?"
He suddenly caught his breath. ...Because the question had hit a bullseye.
"I-I'd never suspect her...!"
"Even if you're not suspicious... you're anxious.@k@vS08/11/321100867. Right?"

"And the cause of that anxiety, of course, was that she was from Hinamizawa.@k@vS08/11/321100869. As much as you say that you deny the rumors about Hinamizawa, in reality you're afraid.@k@vS08/11/321100870. ...Am I wrong?"

"We investigate because we're suspicious of the cause of these incidents, and we're working to find the truth.@k@vS08/11/321100872. ...But you're not like that.@k@vS08/11/321100873. I'm serious here."
Akira finally raised his head, as if repelled.
...Ooishi was no longer smiling. He was casting a stern glare that felt like it was admonishing the boy.

"Am I wrong?@k@vS08/11/321100875. Because all you did was merely suspect her.@k@vS08/11/321100876. You didn't have the courage to believe in her, nor did you make any effort to find the truth... You just ran away.@k@vS08/11/321100877. That's how it looks to me.@k@vS08/11/321100878. ...What do you have to say to that?"
"I... I didn't run away!"
"Then, I'll ask you a question.@k@vS08/11/321100880. Do you truly... want to help that girl?"
"...What do you mean?"

"In other words,@k@vS08/11/321100882.the first thing we need to confirm... is what to do if Natsumi Kimiyoshi turns out to be the same as those crazy people who've committed murder... and take precautions against it."
"As much as you say you're worried about her, you turn a blind eye to that possibility.@k@vS08/11/321100884. ...In that case, can you really say you're trying to understand her...?"
Akira finally recognized his own cowardly and selfish attitude on the matter... and began to shiver.
"...I'll try talking to Natsumi. I'll deal with all of this somehow."
"Chisato..."
"I don't know if it'll go well, though.@k@vS08/37/320300306. ...No, I'll make it go well."
"And when she's back to normal, I'll leave the rest to you, Akira-kun."
"Don't give me that!@k@vS08/37/320300309. I'm asking you to become a proper boyfriend and support her!@k@vS08/37/320300310. You got it?!"
Back then... Chisato made it abundantly clear. She wanted Akira to protect Natsumi.
She declared she'd do her best... because she believed Akira could do it.
Nevertheless... what did he do, himself?
Even though he realized the gravity of her situation, and knew the truth of her troubles... he refused to step forward, out of fear that she would hate and reject him for it.
"Tell me the truth"... would have been a sensible thing to say. If he could, he would have already said it to Natsumi by now, wouldn't he?
In that case, shouldn't he have thought and acted... based around the goal of opening Natsumi's heart?
Not simply appearing concerned for her on the surface and doing a half-assed job of his duties, but rather considering her position and being empathetic...!
Depending on the situation, shouldn't he be saving her, even if it meant relying on the power of this detective standing before him?
"...What should I do?"
"Let me turn that around on you.@k@vS08/11/321100887. ...What do you want to do?"
That was obvious.
He wanted to help Natsumi Kimiyoshi.
He wanted to accept all the problems afflicting her... and believe that they could find a solution, together.
And if so... there was only one thing to do. Muster the courage and compassion to ask Natsumi about her problems...!
".......@k@vS08/39/320200143. I'll talk to Kimiyoshi.@k@vS08/39/320200144. ...Detective, could you give us a little time to deal with this?"
"...Yes, I understand.@k@vS08/11/321100889. Well then, if something comes up, call this number."
Then, Ooishi pulled out a notebook, wrote down the phone number for Kakiuchi Station, and handed it over.
After receiving it, Akira immediately ran out of the park.
"...Sheesh, the energy of youth."
After finishing his last cigarette, Ooishi rose to his feet and stamped it out.
...The dusky sky was already turning from a bold red to a deep blue.
"I hope he'll make it in time..."
He muttered to himself in thought.
Make it in time for what, though?
Perhaps a tragedy was waiting for Akira that would make his hair stand on end.
If so, then what he did just now... was involve the boy in a dangerous situation.
...It made him anxious. However... with Natsumi Kimiyoshi unwilling to open up to anyone, there was truly no other means of getting information out of her.
He examined his surroundings. ...He began to feel like he was being watched.
The crying voice of the higurashi echoed through the entire park.
"Now... Hmm?"
Ooishi suddenly felt a discomfort in his back pocket and pulled out his pager. ...When he examined it closely, he noticed the power was off.
"Ahh, shit.@k@vS08/11/321100895. ...I hope nothing came up."
"...Ah, hello?@k@vS08/11/321100897. Sorry, do you know if anyone was trying to get ahold of me...?"
When Ooishi got in touch with Madoka back at the police station, she rushed straight into her urgent message. Ooishi forgot to even greet her and immediately hung up the receiver.
And then, he leapt out of the phone booth with enough momentum to kick a door down, and chased after Akira without waiting for a police car.
"...Am I cursed?!@k@vS08/11/321100902. Everything I've done has completely backfired...!!"
He had intended to prevent a tragedy... but the situation had already rapidly escalated in ways that Ooishi couldn't have imagined.
...As thing are now, Akira is in serious danger!
He couldn't bear to see... another young person lose their life due to his own actions!!
As Ooishi muttered to himself... he constantly wished for that earnest boy's safety. And he continued running as hard as he could...
